verses.text: Benjamin's line is traced again in detail, beginning with Bela and his sons. The tribe is given special attention. Benjamin, with Judah, would form the heart of the people who returned from exile, so its record is carefully kept.

Benjamin's line is traced again in detail, beginning with Bela and his sons. The tribe is given special attention. Benjamin, with Judah, would form the heart of the people who returned from exile, so its record is carefully kept.
The heads of the fathers at Geba, resettled at Manahath, are named. Even families moved from place to place are tracked. God's account follows His people through every migration and resettlement.
The heads of the fathers at Geba, resettled at Manahath, are named. Even families moved from place to place are tracked. God's account follows His people through every migration and resettlement.
Shaharaim's children, born in Moab, include Shamed, who built Ono and Lod. The genealogy honours the builders of towns. God remembers those who founded the places where His people would later dwell.

The families who dwelt in Aijalon and 'drove away the inhabitants of Gath' are listed. Benjamin's clans were men of action. Faith and courage in claiming the land are noted among the names.

More chief men of Benjamin are recorded, many dwelling in Jerusalem. The tribe had its place in the holy city. To live near the house of God was a privilege rooted in their heritage.

The genealogy continues with the heads of Benjamin's fathers. Name after name is preserved. God keeps a careful record of His people, valuing each family in its place.

These chief men 'dwelt in Jerusalem,' heads of their fathers by their generations. Benjamin shared the holy city with Judah. Nearness to God's chosen place was woven into the tribe's story.

The family at Gibeon is named — the ancestors of king Saul, including a Kish and a Baal. From this household Israel's first king would come. God quietly prepares the lineage of those He will use.

Saul's line is traced: Kish, Saul, Jonathan, and Merib-baal. The house of the rejected king is recorded with honour. Though the throne passed to David, Saul's family is not erased from God's book.

Saul's line is traced: Kish, Saul, Jonathan, and Merib-baal. The house of the rejected king is recorded with honour. Though the throne passed to David, Saul's family is not erased from God's book.

Saul's descendants are followed for many generations, ending with mighty archers. The line of the fallen king lived on. God remembers even the house set aside, preserving the family of Jonathan, David's beloved friend.
